metadata.semantic_description:
Sex: Female
Highly competitive Agreeableness (13th pct) — Competitive, skeptical, challenging
Highly disorganised Conscientiousness (4th pct) — Flexible, spontaneous, disorganised
Introverted Extraversion (27th pct) — Introverted, reserved, solitary
Highly neurotic Neuroticism (80th pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
Highly conventional Openness to Experience (19th pct) — Conventional, practical, down-to-earth
  Very Low Tender-Mindedness (6th pct) — Tough-minded, unsympathetic, objective
  Very Low Achievement Striving (3rd pct) — Easygoing, content with modest achievement
  Very Low Self-Discipline (0th pct) — Procrastinates, easily distracted
  Moderate Activity Level (41st pct)
  Very Low Excitement Seeking (14th pct) — Cautious, avoids stimulation
  Very Low Positive Emotions (16th pct) — Less exuberant, serious
  High Anger / Hostility (74th pct) — Easily angered, irritable, hostile
  High Self-Consciousness (75th pct) — Self-conscious, shy, easily embarrassed
  Highly neurotic Neuroticism (84th pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
  Moderate Chronological Age (54th pct)

message.reasoning: We are simulating a baseline opinion on "Trust In Mainstream Journalism" for a 54-year-old female with high neuroticism, low conscientiousness, low openness, low agreeableness (especially trust), and low extraversion. The profile indicates very low trust (agreeableness trust at 16th percentile), high anxiety and hostility, and low positive emotions. Also low tender-mindedness (tough-minded). She is skeptical, anxious, and not trusting of others. This strongly suggests a baseline of distrust in mainstream journalism. However, we must consider the scale: -1000 is maximal opposition (categorically rejects). Given her traits, she likely leans strongly against trusting mainstream journalism. But not necessarily maximal because she is not necessarily a campaigner. Low conscientiousness suggests she might not engage deeply with news, and low openness suggests she prefers conventional sources maybe? But the high neuroticism and low trust lead to skepticism. Let's aim for a score around -600 to -800. I'll pick -682 as a non-round number. Confidence moderately high but not extremely high because we don't have explicit political identity. Reasoning: very low trust and high neuroticism with anxiety/hostility produce baseline skepticism.
